content.problemStatement:
You are given a string SSS of length NNN, containing lowercase Latin letters. You are also given an integer KKK.

You would like to create a new string S′S'S′ by following the following process:


First, partition SSS into exactly KKK non-empty subsequences S1,S2,…,SKS_1, S_2, \ldots, S_KS1​,S2​,…,SK​. Note that every character of SSS must be present in exactly one of the SiS_iSi​.
Then, create S′S'S′ as the concatenation of these subsequences, i.e, S′=S1+S2+…+SKS' = S_1 + S_2 + \ldots + S_KS′=S1​+S2​+…+SK​, where +++ denotes string concatenation.
Determine the **lexicographically** smallest S′S'S′ that can be created.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers, NNN and KKK.
The second line of each test case contains the string SSS.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the lexicographically smallest string S′S'S′ that can be created.
constraints: SSS contains only lowercase Latin letters.
Sum of NNN over all cases won't exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 111: K=1K = 1K=1, so our only option is to set S′=S1=whizzyS' = S_1 = whizzyS′=S1​=whizzy.
Test Case 222: Partition S=aaacdecdebacdeS = \textcolor{red}{aaa}\textcolor{blue}{cdecdeb}\textcolor{red}{ac}\textcolor{blue}{de}S=aaacdecdebacde into S1=aaaacS_1 = \textcolor{red}{aaaac}S1​=aaaac and S2=cdecdebdeS_2 = \textcolor{blue}{cdecdebde}S2​=cdecdebde, to form S′=aaaaccdecdebdeS' = aaaaccdecdebdeS′=aaaaccdecdebde.
Test Case 333: Partition S=dbcaS = d\textcolor{blue}{bc}\textcolor{red}{a}S=dbca into S1=aS_1 = \textcolor{red}{a}S1​=a, S2=bcS_2 = \textcolor{blue}{bc}S2​=bc, and S3=dS_3 = dS3​=d to form S′=abcdS' = abcdS′=abcd.
In both test cases 222 and 333, it can be shown that no other partition gives a lexicographically smaller S′S'S′.
